title: Chapter 1
text: Joan D. Vinge received a degree in anthropology from San Diego State University in 1971 and considers herself an “anthropologist of the future.” She worked briefly as a salvage archaeologist before turning to writing. Her novella Eyes of Amber won the Hugo Award in 1978, and her stories “Fireship” and “View from a Height” were Hugo nominees in 1979. Ms. Vinge lives in Brooklyn , New York .
